what: Dynamic decode context (bucketed KV read) + sliding-window KV read for the fast-verify path. Single-box microbench isolates the decode-cost wins; live 3x scattered-4090 swarm confirms the engine + a clean tok/s baseline on today's links.
swarm: 3 scattered RTX 4090 stages over WAN (CA head -> WA -> NC tail) + layerless coord/draft (Utah); mesh-optimal ring loop 185 ms

1_dynamic_context_bucketing.change: StaticKV.update returns k/v[:, :, :alen] where alen = smallest DECODE_BUCKET >= current ctx (2048,4096,8192,...), not maxlen. The dropped tail is unwritten/causal-masked, so the read is a PREFIX -> BIT-IDENTICAL to the full-buffer read (same matmul tiling + trailing exact-zeros).

microbench_ms_per_step_short_ctx_~200_on.verdict: decode cost is now FLAT in the ceiling (set by live ctx, not max_ctx). Old behavior grew with max_ctx: a short prompt under a 64k ceiling cost 24.87 ms/step; bucketed costs 3.42 -> ~7.3x. The 'pay-for-your-ceiling' tax is gone, bit-exactly.

2_sliding_window_kv_read.change: gpt-oss alternates full-attention and sliding-window(128) layers. In window_mode the sliding layers index_select ONLY the wlen=win+kp1-1 keys their window can attend (a fixed-length, graph-capturable gather), instead of reading the full bucket and masking. O(window) not O(ctx).
2_sliding_window_kv_read.correctness: math proven bit-exact in isolation (max|full-window|=0.0; identical absolute positions attended). In the full model it is NUMERICALLY equivalent but NOT bit-identical: the shifted gather re-tiles the AV matmul -> ~few-ULP FP non-associativity (layer 0/2 bit-identical, layer 4 ~4 ULP) that MoE expert-routing amplifies over autoregressive steps (same class as the documented cross-K FP non-determinism; each output is a valid greedy decode).
2_sliding_window_kv_read.default: OFF (FV_WINDOW=1 to opt in per node) -- keeps the default engine bit-exact

microbench_ms_per_step_one_6layer_stage_.note: speedup grows with ctx; only the sliding half of layers benefit, MoE/MLP cost unchanged -> approaches its asymptote at longer ctx

rerun_max_ctx_8192_4x_ceiling.verdict: a 4x larger context ceiling (8192 vs 2048) cost NOTHING -> 27.63 tok/s, >= the 2048 baseline. This is the dynamic-context win end-to-end: the ceiling no longer taxes decode. Old (non-bucketed) code at max_ctx=8192 reads 8192 keys/step (microbench 5.53 vs 3.51 ms/step). depth=4 is the sweet spot; depth=8 over-fills (+56 stale, no gain).

3_swarm_runs.regime_note: lower than the ~40 receipt because today's links give a 185 ms ring loop vs the receipt's in-region 102 ms (recv ~90-160 ms dominates). At short ctx the swarm is WAN-latency-bound: per-step decode (~3.5 ms) << ring recv (~100 ms). So the decode-compute wins move LONG-context tok/s and remove the max_ctx tax, not the short-ctx WAN-bound number. The ~24->28 band across runs is WAN variance, not the bucketing.

files: phase0/fastverify.py (DECODE_BUCKETS bucketing; StaticKV window read; FV_WINDOW gate)
research/iso_mask.py + research/fv_layerdiff.py (root-causing the window FP non-associativity)
honest_status: Dynamic context (bucketing) is a clean, bit-exact, always-on win: decode cost no longer scales with the ceiling. Sliding-window read is a 1.2-1.44x long-ctx decode lever, numerically-equivalent but not bit-identical (opt-in). On WAN-bound links the end-to-end short-ctx tok/s is set by ring latency, so these are long-context / memory-wall wins.
